Job Role Description: The Clinical Practitioner provides professional and client focused therapeutic services to individuals couples children and families experiencing emotional psychological and relationship challenges. The role supports clients to set goals build coping and communication skills and promote positive behavioural change and improved mental wellbeing. The Clinical Practitioner delivers high quality services in line with professional standards legislative requirements and Centacare values while contributing to collaborative practice accurate documentation and ongoing professional development.
Job Role Skills and Experience: •    Accredited education and training in psychology, social work, counselling, mental health or a similar discipline leading to a professional qualification that is recognised in Australia•    Membership with a relevant professional body (such as AASW, APHRA, PACFA)•    Experience and skills in provision direct of clinical services. Demonstrable experience in working with children, adolescents, and adults to address issues such as emotional and psychological trauma, anxiety, depression, life-stage issues, behavioural problems etc. •    Demonstrable ability in implementing intervention strategies based on sound theoretical frameworks. •    Experience and demonstrable skills in running group programmes with early intervention and wellbeing promotion focus •    Demonstrable experience and ability in liaising and working with stakeholders such as referring agencies•    Skills and experience in psychometric assessment of children •    Experience in working with couples and families within a relationship framework •    Skills and experience in psychosocial assessment, case formulation, and intervention planning•    Willingness to work within the Catholic identity and ethos of the organisation

Job Role Description:
The General Manager oversees the Manufacturing Teams performance, driving business growth and delivering on financial targets for the Australian region. This position contributes to and executes strategic plans aligning with the owners vision, while cultivating an environment of creativity, innovation, and continuous improvement. They will also facilitate and oversee the digital transformation of the business, leveraging industry 4.0 and 5.0 advancements and ensuring that the business is at the forefront of the industrys technological trajectory.

Duties and responsibilities include:


	Direct the performance of the Australian manufacturing teams, ensuring effectiveness and productivity.
	Develop and implement robust growth strategies, focusing on enhancing productivity, capability and improving quality.
	Spearhead the digital transformation of the business in alignment with industry 4.0 and 5.0 frameworks.
	Establish, implement, and maintain company policies and procedures.
	Manage HR systems and processes, including people development, performance management and succession planning.
	Ensure regulatory compliance with OHS, Quality, and Environmental standards and manage regulatory body relationships.
	Provide leadership and strategic direction to drive business growth.
	Facilitate professional development opportunities and coaching for team members.
	Act as a primary point of escalation and company representative for major projects.
	Collaborate with the Consultancyand Operations teams to achieve business targets.
	Lead the Manufacturingteam in supporting project timelines and addressing operational issues.
Job Role Skills and Experience:
At least 15 years of leadership experience in a manufacturing or related industry, preferably within the art or creative sector.
	Proven strategic planning, financial planning, and budgeting skills.
	Comprehensive understanding of modern manufacturing techniques and digital transformation, including industry 4.0 and 5.0.
	Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment, managing multiple priorities while focusing on strategic objectives.
	Experience in HR systems and processes, quality control, and environmental compliance.
	High ethical standards, accountability, and commitment to transparency.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to foster relationships across all levels.
	Experience leading and managing a multi-disciplinary team to achieve business objectives.
	Outstanding commercial skills and business acumen.
	Passion for art and its societal role, and resilience to handle pressure in a growing business.
	A commitment to continuously improving processes and practices in alignment with industry trends and standards.

Job Role Description:
The Rostering Officer at Lutheran Disability Services plays a crucial role in ensuring that the staffing needs of the organization are met effectively and efficiently. This position involves coordinating and managing staff schedules to provide optimal support for clients while maintaining compliance with relevant regulations and policies.

Key Responsibilities:


	Develop, implement, and maintain effective rostering systems that align with service delivery requirements.
	Coordinate staff rosters to ensure adequate coverage across various programs and services.
	Monitor attendance, leave requests, and shift changes to optimize workforce allocation.
	Communicate with staff regarding roster changes, availability, and any scheduling issues.
	Collaborate with management to address staffing shortages or surpluses and adjust rosters accordingly.
	Maintain accurate records of staff hours worked, leave balances, and other relevant data.


The successful candidate will be proactive in problem-solving, possess strong organizational skills, and demonstrate a commitment to providing high-quality service to both staff and clients within the disability sector.
Job Role Skills and Experience:
Required skills


	Strong organizational and time management skills
	Proficient in rostering software and scheduling tools
	Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities
	Attention to detail and ability to maintain accuracy under pressure
	Problem-solving skills and the ability to think critically
	Ability to work collaboratively within a team environment
	Understanding of relevant legislation and compliance requirements in disability services


Required experiences


	A minimum of 2 years experience in a rostering or scheduling role, preferably within the healthcare or disability sector
	Experience with workforce management systems or similar scheduling software
	Demonstrated experience in managing staff rosters effectively while adhering to budget constraints
	Previous experience working with diverse teams and understanding their needs in service delivery settings
	Familiarity with employee relations, including handling conflicts or issues related to staffing schedules

Job Role Description: Position Description:The Learning and Development Manager at golddemosite21 plays a pivotal role in enhancing the skills and capabilities of our workforce. This position is responsible for designing, implementing, and overseeing training programmes that align with the company's strategic objectives. The successful candidate will work closely with various departments to identify training needs and develop tailored solutions to foster employee growth.Key Responsibilities:Conduct comprehensive training needs assessments across all departments to identify skill gaps and development opportunities.Design and deliver engaging learning experiences through various formats such as workshops, e-learning modules, and on-the-job training.Develop a cohesive Learning and Development strategy that supports employee career progression while aligning with organisational goals.Evaluate the effectiveness of training programmes through feedback mechanisms, assessments, and performance metrics to ensure continuous improvement.Collaborate with external training providers to enhance internal offerings and keep abreast of industry trends in Learning and Development.Create an inclusive learning environment that encourages participation from all employees regardless of their background or experience levels.Manage the Learning Management System (LMS) to track employee progress, maintain records of completed trainings, and generate reports for management review.Advise leadership on talent development strategies to support succession planning initiatives within the organisation.The ideal candidate will demonstrate a passion for fostering a culture of continuous learning, possess strong interpersonal skills, and be adept at managing multiple projects simultaneously. A proactive approach in identifying areas for improvement within current programmes will be crucial for success in this role.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Required skillsStrong understanding of learning theories and instructional design principlesExcellent communication and interpersonal skillsProficiency in using Learning Management Systems (LMS) and e-learning toolsAbility to analyse training needs and evaluate programme effectivenessProject management skills to oversee the development and implementation of training initiativesCreativity in designing engaging training materials and programmesStrong organisational skills with attention to detailAbility to work collaboratively across departments and levels within the organisationRequired experiencesA minimum of 5 years of experience in a Learning and Development role, preferably in a managerial capacityDemonstrated experience in designing, delivering, and assessing training programmes for diverse audiencesPrevious experience in conducting training needs assessments and evaluationsExperience in managing budgets for training initiatives and projectsProven track record of implementing successful learning strategies that align with business objectivesExperience working with senior stakeholders to develop tailored learning solutionsA background in adult education or human resources is preferred
